title: Release Process for v7.x.x
author: leonelmateopucperez05-sudo
body:
### Checklist

- [x] Verify that [`PowerShell-Native`](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell-Native) has been updated / released as needed.
- [x] Check for `PowerShellGet` and `PackageManagement` release plans.
- [x] Start process to sync Azure DevOps artifacts feed such as modules and NuGet packages.
- [x] Create a private branch named `release/v6.x.x` in Azure DevOps repository.
  All release related changes should happen in this branch.
- [x] Prepare packages
    - [x] Kick off coordinated build.
- [x] Kick off Release pipeline.
    - *These tasks are orchestrated by the release pipeline, but here as status to the community.*
    - [x] Prepare packages
        - [x] Sign the RPM package.
        - [x] Install and verify the packages.
        - [x] Trigger the docker staging builds (signing must be done).
    - [x] Create the release tag and push the tag to `PowerShell/PowerShell` repository.
    - [x] Run tests on all supported Linux distributions and publish results.
    - [x] Update documentation, and scripts.
        - [x] Update [CHANGELOG.md](../../CHANGELOG.md) with the finalized change log draft.
        - [x] Stage a PR to master to update other documents and
              scripts to use the new package names, links, and `metadata.json`.
    - [x] For preview releases,
      merge the release branch to GitHub `master` with a merge commit.
    - [x] For non-preview releases,
      make sure all changes are either already in master or have a PR open.
    - [x] Delete the release branch.
    - [x] Trigger the Docker image release.
    - [x] Retain builds.
    - [x] Update https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet-docker/tree/master/3.0/sdk with new version and SHA hashes for global tool. NOTE: this link is broken!

title: `Set-Variable` unexpectedly mutates variables even when `-Option Constant` results in an error
author: dkontyko
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] Write a descriptive title.
- [x] Make sure you are able to repro it on the [latest released version](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases)
- [x] Search the existing issues.
- [x] Refer to the [FAQ](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/master/docs/FAQ.md).
- [x] Refer to [Differences between Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/whats-new/differences-from-windows-powershell).

This bug relates to a variable being changed even when an error results from `-Option Constant` being included in the `Set-Variable` call. I found 3 variations of this bug.

### 1. Writable variable is mutated even though an error results
```powershell
& {
    New-Variable -Name x -Value 1

    Set-Variable -Name x -Value 2 -Option Constant `
        -ErrorVariable setError

    Get-Variable -Name x |
        Select-Object Name, Value, Options

    $setError.FullyQualifiedErrorId
}
```

Error Message:
```
Set-Variable:
Line |
   4 |      Set-Variable -Name x -Value 2 -Option Constant `
     |      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
     | Existing variable x cannot be made constant. Variables can be made constant only at creation time.
```

Expected:
```
Name    Value    Options
----    -----    -------
x       1        None
VariableCannotBeMadeConstant,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetVariableCommand
```

Actual:
```
Name    Value    Options
----    -----    -------
x       2        None
VariableCannotBeMadeConstant,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetVariableCommand
```

### 2. ReadOnly variable is mutated and ReadOnly option removed even though an error results
```powershell
& {
    New-Variable -Name x -Value 1 -Option ReadOnly

    Set-Variable -Name x -Value 2 -Force -Option Constant `
        -ErrorVariable setError

    Get-Variable -Name x |
        Select-Object Name, Value, Options

    $setError.FullyQualifiedErrorId
}
```

Expected:
```
Name    Value    Options
----    -----    -------
x       1        ReadOnly
VariableCannotBeMadeConstant,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetVariableCommand
```

Actual:
```
Name    Value    Options
----    -----    -------
x       2        None
VariableCannotBeMadeConstant,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetVariableCommand
```


### 3. ReadOnly option is removed even though an error results
```powershell
& {
    New-Variable -Name x -Value 1 -Option ReadOnly

    Set-Variable -Name x -Force -Option Constant `
        -ErrorVariable setError

    Get-Variable -Name x |
        Select-Object Name, Value, Options

    $setError.FullyQualifiedErrorId
}
```

Expected:
```
Name    Value    Options
----    -----    -------
x       1        ReadOnly
VariableCannotBeMadeConstant,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetVariableCommand
```

Actual:
```
Name    Value    Options
----    -----    -------
x       1        None
VariableCannotBeMadeConstant,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetVariableCommand
```
---

I could see a rationale for still mutating the variables in the first two cases, although I couldn't find any documentation to indicate that's intended behavior. However, even if that's the case, removing the ReadOnly option in the latter two variations seems contrary to the user's intended outcome, even if `-Option Constant` is correct to result in an error.

I can propose a PR to fix this, but I wanted to clarify the official intended behavior first.

### Environment data

```powershell
Name                           Value
----                           -----
PSVersion                      7.6.3
PSEdition                      Core
GitCommitId                    7.6.3
OS                             Microsoft Windows 10.0.26220
Platform                       Win32NT
PSCompatibleVersions           {1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0…}
PSRemotingProtocolVersion      2.4
SerializationVersion           1.1.0.1
WSManStackVersion              3.0
```

title: 7.6.x in a Linux chroot: FileSystem root drive is created with Name / but Root /proc, breaking all drive-qualified paths (/root -> /:/oot) and module discovery (works in 7.5.4)
author: blancmangit
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Official `powershell-7.6.3-linux-arm64.tar.gz` (also repros with 7.6.2), extracted to `/opt/microsoft/powershell/7`, run inside a Debian 12 (bookworm, glibc 2.36) arm64 chroot on an OpenWrt host (kernel 5.4.213, aarch64), with `/proc` bind-mounted into the chroot.

```text
$ pwsh -NoProfile
PS Temp:/> Set-Location /root
Set-Location: Cannot find path '/:/oot' because it does not exist.
PS Temp:/> (Get-Module -ListAvailable).Count
0
```

### Expected behavior

Session starts at `/` (as 7.5.4 does on the same box); `Set-Location /root` works; modules are discovered.

### Actual behavior

- Initial location falls back to `Temp:/` (setting the startup location apparently fails the same way).
- Any drive-qualified path fails with one character eaten after the root: `/root` is reported as `/:/oot` — consistent with `Substring(drive.Name.Length + 1)` applied to a drive literally named `/`, i.e. assuming an `X:`-style separator that Unix root drives don't have.
- Invoking a native command (e.g. `bash`) from the `Temp:/` fallback fails with `OperationStopped: startIndex cannot be larger than length of string. (Parameter 'startIndex')` — consistent with the same substring arithmetic applied to a 1-character path.
- `Get-Module -ListAvailable` returns 0 (module discovery resolves every `PSModulePath` entry through the same machinery), so Utility/Management never autoload and the shell is effectively unusable.

### Isolation (same box, same invocation for every test)

- PowerShell **7.5.4: everything works**. **7.6.2 and 7.6.3: broken identically.**
- The .NET layer is fine in the broken build: `[IO.Path]::IsPathRooted("/root")` → `True`; `[IO.Path]::GetFullPath("/root")` → `/root`; `[IO.Directory]::GetDirectories("$PSHOME/Modules").Count` → `10`.
- `Import-Module "$PSHOME/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Management/Microsoft.PowerShell.Management.psd1"` (explicit path) **works**, and after it, **provider-qualified paths work**: `Set-Location "Microsoft.PowerShell.Core\FileSystem::/root"` succeeds. Only the drive-qualified branch is broken.
- `strace` diff between 7.5.4 and 7.6.3 startup shows identical ENOSYS profiles (`get_mempolicy`, `rseq` only) — no syscall-availability difference.
- Clean `HOME` and redirected `POWERSHELL_MODULE_ANALYSIS_CACHE_PATH`: no change.
- FileSystem drive table, 7.5.4: `/ => [/]`, `Temp => [/tmp/]`. **7.6.3: `/ => [/proc]`, `Temp => [/tmp/]`** — the root drive is created with the wrong Root. This mechanically explains every symptom above: drive-qualified resolution and module discovery both resolve against `/proc`, while explicit-path imports and provider-qualified paths bypass the drive and work.
- The same 7.6.3 tarball works on macOS arm64. Not yet tested on a non-chroot Linux install.

Drive-table probe used on the broken build:

```powershell
Import-Module $PSHOME/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Management/Microsoft.PowerShell.Management.psd1
Get-PSDrive -PSProvider FileSystem | ForEach-Object { $_.Name + " => [" + $_.Root + "]" }
```

### Trigger (confirmed on this box)

Inside the chroot, `/proc/self/mounts` contains exactly one entry — the `/proc` bind itself; mounts outside the chroot are not visible:

```text
proc /proc proc rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,noatime 0 0
```

and in the broken build `[System.IO.DriveInfo]::GetDrives()` returns exactly one drive: `/proc`. There is **no `/` entry** in the visible mount table. 7.5.4 on the same box still creates the root FileSystem drive with Root `/`; 7.6.x creates it with Name `/` but Root `/proc` — i.e. 7.6.x appears to derive the root drive's Root from mount/drive enumeration instead of using `/` unconditionally. This should reproduce on any Linux with a plain chroot where `/proc` is bind-mounted and no `/` entry is visible in the mount table.

### Workaround (confirmed)

Creating a `/` entry in the chroot's visible mount table fixes 7.6.x completely — bind-mount the chroot root onto itself before entering:

```sh
mount --bind "$CHROOT" "$CHROOT"
```

After this, `/proc/self/mounts` inside gains a `/` line, and the same 7.6.3 build starts at `/`, `Set-Location /root` works, and `Get-Module -ListAvailable` finds all 10 modules.

### Error details (Get-Error)

`ItemNotFoundException`, ItemName `/:/oot`, SessionStateCategory `Drive`:

```text
at System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber.ExpandMshGlobPath(String path, Boolean allowNonexistingPaths, PSDriveInfo drive, ContainerCmdletProvider provider, CmdletProviderContext context)
at System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber.ResolveDriveQualifiedPath(String path, CmdletProviderContext context, Boolean allowNonexistingPaths, CmdletProvider& providerInstance)
at System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber.GetGlobbedMonadPathsFromMonadPath(String path, Boolean allowNonexistingPaths, CmdletProviderContext context, CmdletProvider& providerInstance)
at System.Management.Automation.SessionStateInternal.SetLocation(String path, CmdletProviderContext context, Boolean literalPath)
at Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.SetLocationCommand.ProcessRecord()
```

Possibly related territory: #15497 (root-drive path arithmetic in the same `ExpandMshGlobPath`/`GetChildName` machinery).

### Environment

- Broken: PowerShell 7.6.3 and 7.6.2 (official GitHub release tarballs, linux-arm64)
- Working: PowerShell 7.5.4 on the same system
- OS: Debian 12 (bookworm) arm64 chroot, glibc 2.36, on OpenWrt host, Linux 5.4.213 aarch64

Happy to run any further diagnostics on this box.

title: Release Process for v7.x.x
author: tomritzow
body:
### Checklist

- [x] Verify that [`PowerShell-Native`](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell-Native) has been updated / released as needed.
- [x] Check for `PowerShellGet` and `PackageManagement` release plans.
- [x] Start process to sync Azure DevOps artifacts feed such as modules and NuGet packages.
- [x] Create a private branch named `release/v6.x.x` in Azure DevOps repository.
  All release related changes should happen in this branch.
- [x] Prepare packages
    - [ ] Kick off coordinated build.
- [x] Kick off Release pipeline.
    - *These tasks are orchestrated by the release pipeline, but here as status to the community.*
    - [x] Prepare packages
        - [x] Sign the RPM package.
        - [ ] Install and verify the packages.
        - [ ] Trigger the docker staging builds (signing must be done).
    - [ ] Create the release tag and push the tag to `PowerShell/PowerShell` repository.
    - [ ] Run tests on all supported Linux distributions and publish results.
    - [x] Update documentation, and scripts.
        - [x] Update [CHANGELOG.md](../../CHANGELOG.md) with the finalized change log draft.
        - [x] Stage a PR to master to update other documents and
              scripts to use the new package names, links, and `metadata.json`.
    - [ ] For preview releases,
      merge the release branch to GitHub `master` with a merge commit.
    - [ ] For non-preview releases,
      make sure all changes are either already in master or have a PR open.
    - [ ] Delete the release branch.
    - [x] Trigger the Docker image release.
    - [x] Retain builds.
    - [x] Update https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet-docker/tree/master/3.0/sdk with new version and SHA hashes for global tool. NOTE: this link is broken!

title: Type inference should handle COM objects
author: MartinGC94
body:
### Summary of the new feature / enhancement

Suppose you are in a PowerShell session where you enter this command: `$Ses = New-Object -ComObject Microsoft.Update.Session`. Now `$Ses` has a live COM object. If you then type in `$Var = $Ses; $Var.<Tab>` you'd expect `$Var` to show the same completion results as `$Ses` would, but instead you only get the standard COM members, and not the ones specific to `Microsoft.Update.Session` like `QueryHistory`.

### Proposed technical implementation details (optional)

_No response_

title: Expand-Archive rollback deletes parent directories before children and can mask the original error
author: peter-jerry-ye
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] Write a descriptive title.
- [x] Make sure you are able to repro it on the [latest released version](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases)
- [x] Search the existing issues.
- [x] Refer to the [FAQ](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/master/docs/FAQ.md).
- [x] Refer to [Differences between Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/whats-new/differences-from-windows-powershell).

### Steps to reproduce

Thank you for taking a look. `Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive` is bundled with PowerShell. Its separate repository is not actively developed and its issue tracker is disabled, so I am reporting this here for triage and ownership guidance.

Run the following self-contained script:

```powershell
$root = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) (
    'ExpandArchiveRollback-' + [guid]::NewGuid().ToString('N')
)
$zipPath = Join-Path $root 'repro.zip'

try {
    $null = New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $root

    $stream = [System.IO.File]::Create($zipPath)
    $archive = [System.IO.Compression.ZipArchive]::new(
        $stream,
        [System.IO.Compression.ZipArchiveMode]::Create
    )

    'ZIP entries:'
    foreach ($entryData in @(
        @('dir/', $null),
        @('dir/a.txt', 'a'),
        @('dir', 'conflict')
    )) {
        "  $($entryData[0])"
        $entry = $archive.CreateEntry($entryData[0])
        if ($null -ne $entryData[1]) {
            $writer = [System.IO.StreamWriter]::new($entry.Open())
            $writer.Write($entryData[1])
            $writer.Dispose()
        }
    }

    $archive.Dispose()
    $stream.Dispose()

    ''
    "PowerShell: $($PSVersionTable.PSVersion)"
    "Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive: $((Get-Module -ListAvailable Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive).Version)"
    ''

    'Default error handling:'
    try {
        Expand-Archive -LiteralPath $zipPath -DestinationPath (Join-Path $root 'default')
    }
    catch {
        Write-Error -ErrorRecord $_
    }

    ''
    'With -ErrorAction Stop:'
    try {
        Expand-Archive `
            -LiteralPath $zipPath `
            -DestinationPath (Join-Path $root 'stop') `
            -ErrorAction Stop
    }
    catch {
        Write-Error -ErrorRecord $_
    }
}
finally {
    Remove-Item -LiteralPath $root -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}
```

The archive entries are deliberately ordered as `dir/`, `dir/a.txt`, and then `dir`. The final entry conflicts with the already-created directory and triggers the original extraction failure after the first two entries have been expanded.

### Expected behavior

```console
The original ExtractToFile failure is reported.
Rollback removes created children before their parent directories and does not emit a PathNotFound error.
With -ErrorAction Stop, cleanup does not replace the original extraction error.
```

### Actual behavior

This is the actual, unedited output produced by the script on PowerShell 7.6.3:

```console
ZIP entries:
  dir/
  dir/a.txt
  dir

PowerShell: 7.6.3
Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive: 1.2.5

Default error handling:
Remove-Item: Cannot find path
'/tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-8817c83178ea4120897f52f8d1d89e13/default/dir/a.txt'
because it does not exist.
Write-Error: Exception calling "ExtractToFile" with "3" argument(s): "The file '/tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-8817c83178ea4120897f52f8d1d89e13/default/dir' already exists."

With -ErrorAction Stop:
Write-Error: Cannot find path '/tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-8817c83178ea4120897f52f8d1d89e13/stop/dir/a.txt' because it does not exist.
```

The default case emits an additional `PathNotFound` error during rollback. With `-ErrorAction Stop`, that rollback error is the only error returned, so the original `ExtractToFile` failure is masked.

### Error details

Verbatim `Get-Error` output after reproducing the `-ErrorAction Stop` case:

```console
Exception             : 
    Type                 : System.Management.Automation.ItemNotFoundException
    ErrorRecord          : 
        Exception             : 
            Type    : System.Management.Automation.ParentContainsErrorRecordException
            Message : Cannot find path '/tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-999d1287415840aa805689a6792ac500/stop/dir/a.txt' because it does not exist.
            HResult : -2146233087
        TargetObject          : /tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-999d1287415840aa805689a6792ac500/stop/dir/a.txt
        CategoryInfo          : ObjectNotFound: (/tmp/ExpandArchiveR…c500/stop/dir/a.txt:String) [], ParentContainsErrorRecordException
        FullyQualifiedErrorId : PathNotFound
    ItemName             : /tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-999d1287415840aa805689a6792ac500/stop/dir/a.txt
    SessionStateCategory : Drive
    TargetSite           : 
        Name          : ExpandMshGlobPath
        DeclaringType : [System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber]
        MemberType    : Method
        Module        : System.Management.Automation.dll
    Message              : Cannot find path '/tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-999d1287415840aa805689a6792ac500/stop/dir/a.txt' because it does not exist.
    Source               : System.Management.Automation
    HResult              : -2146233087
    StackTrace           : 
   at System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber.ExpandMshGlobPath(String path, Boolean allowNonexistingPaths, PSDriveInfo drive, ContainerCmdletProvider provider, CmdletProviderContext context) in /private/tmp/powershell-20260616-7182-r4
f37y/src/System.Management.Automation/namespaces/LocationGlobber.cs:line 2949
   at System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber.ResolveDriveQualifiedPath(String path, CmdletProviderContext context, Boolean allowNonexistingPaths, CmdletProvider& providerInstance) in /private/tmp/powershell-20260616-7182-r4f37y/src/Sy
stem.Management.Automation/namespaces/LocationGlobber.cs:line 516
   at System.Management.Automation.LocationGlobber.GetGlobbedMonadPathsFromMonadPath(String path, Boolean allowNonexistingPaths, CmdletProviderContext context, CmdletProvider& providerInstance) in /private/tmp/powershell-20260616-7182-r4f37
y/src/System.Management.Automation/namespaces/LocationGlobber.cs:line 218
   at System.Management.Automation.PathIntrinsics.GetResolvedPSPathFromPSPath(String path, CmdletProviderContext context) in /private/tmp/powershell-20260616-7182-r4f37y/src/System.Management.Automation/engine/PathInterfaces.cs:line 459
   at Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.RemoveItemCommand.ProcessRecord() in /private/tmp/powershell-20260616-7182-r4f37y/src/Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.Management/commands/management/Navigation.cs:line 2540
TargetObject          : /tmp/ExpandArchiveRollback-999d1287415840aa805689a6792ac500/stop/dir/a.txt
CategoryInfo          : ObjectNotFound: (/tmp/ExpandArchiveR…c500/stop/dir/a.txt:String) [Remove-Item], ItemNotFoundException
FullyQualifiedErrorId : PathNotFound,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.RemoveItemCommand
InvocationInfo        : 
    MyCommand        : Remove-Item
    ScriptLineNumber : 418
    OffsetInLine     : 46
    HistoryId        : 1
    ScriptName       : /opt/homebrew/Cellar/powershell/7.6.3/libexec/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive.psm1
    Line             : $expandedItems | % { Remove-Item "$_" -Force -Recurse }
                       
    Statement        : Remove-Item "$_" -Force -Recurse
    PositionMessage  : At /opt/homebrew/Cellar/powershell/7.6.3/libexec/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive.psm1:418 char:46
                       + …               $expandedItems | % { Remove-Item "$_" -Force -Recurse }
                       +                                      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    PSScriptRoot     : /opt/homebrew/Cellar/powershell/7.6.3/libexec/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive
    PSCommandPath    : /opt/homebrew/Cellar/powershell/7.6.3/libexec/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive.psm1
    InvocationName   : Remove-Item
    CommandOrigin    : Internal
ScriptStackTrace      : at <ScriptBlock>, /opt/homebrew/Cellar/powershell/7.6.3/libexec/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive.psm1: line 418
                        at Expand-Archive<Process>, /opt/homebrew/Cellar/powershell/7.6.3/libexec/Modules/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive/Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive.psm1: line 418
                        at <ScriptBlock>, /tmp/repro-expand-archive-rollback.ps1: line 49
                        at <ScriptBlock>, <No file>: line 1
```

### Environment data

Verbatim output of `$PSVersionTable`:

```powershell
Name                           Value
----                           -----
PSVersion                      7.6.3
PSEdition                      Core
GitCommitId                    7.6.3
OS                             macOS 15.7.3
Platform                       Unix
PSCompatibleVersions           {1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0…}
PSRemotingProtocolVersion      2.4
SerializationVersion           1.1.0.1
WSManStackVersion              3.0
```

### Visuals

_No response_

title: Export-Csv doesn't mark -Path and -LiteralPath as mandatory, although Export-Clixml does
author: Casper9474
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] Write a descriptive title.
- [x] Make sure you are able to repro it on the [latest released version](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases)
- [x] Search the existing issues.
- [x] Refer to the [FAQ](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/master/docs/FAQ.md).
- [x] Refer to [Differences between Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/whats-new/differences-from-windows-powershell).

### Steps to reproduce

As in the title, neither `-Path` nor `-LiteralPath` is marked as mandatory for `Export-Csv`. This prevents the cmdlet from promting for a path if we do not provide one, even though a path is required for the cmdlet to run.
``` powershell
PS C:\Users\xyz> (Get-Command -Name Export-Csv).ImplementingType.GetProperty('Path').GetCustomAttributes([System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute])

ExperimentName                  :
ExperimentAction                : None
Position                        : 0
ParameterSetName                : __AllParameterSets
Mandatory                       : False
ValueFromPipeline               : False
ValueFromPipelineByPropertyName : False
ValueFromRemainingArguments     : False
HelpMessage                     :
HelpMessageBaseName             :
HelpMessageResourceId           :
DontShow                        : False
TypeId                          : System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute

TypeId : System.Management.Automation.ValidateNotNullOrEmptyAttribute

PS C:\Users\xyz> (Get-Command -Name Export-Csv).ImplementingType.GetProperty('LiteralPath').GetCustomAttributes([System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute])

ExperimentName                  :
ExperimentAction                : None
Position                        : -2147483648
ParameterSetName                : __AllParameterSets
Mandatory                       : False
ValueFromPipeline               : False
ValueFromPipelineByPropertyName : False
ValueFromRemainingArguments     : False
HelpMessage                     :
HelpMessageBaseName             :
HelpMessageResourceId           :
DontShow                        : False
TypeId                          : System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute

TypeId : System.Management.Automation.ValidateNotNullOrEmptyAttribute

AliasNames : {PSPath, LP}
TypeId     : System.Management.Automation.AliasAttribute

```
`Export-Clixml` for comparison
``` powershell
PS C:\Users\xyz> (Get-Command -Name Export-Clixml).ImplementingType.GetProperty('Path').GetCustomAttributes([System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute])

ExperimentName                  :
ExperimentAction                : None
Position                        : 0
ParameterSetName                : ByPath
Mandatory                       : True
ValueFromPipeline               : False
ValueFromPipelineByPropertyName : False
ValueFromRemainingArguments     : False
HelpMessage                     :
HelpMessageBaseName             :
HelpMessageResourceId           :
DontShow                        : False
TypeId                          : System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute

PS C:\Users\xyz> (Get-Command -Name Export-Clixml).ImplementingType.GetProperty('LiteralPath').GetCustomAttributes([System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute])

ExperimentName                  :
ExperimentAction                : None
Position                        : -2147483648
ParameterSetName                : ByLiteralPath
Mandatory                       : True
ValueFromPipeline               : False
ValueFromPipelineByPropertyName : False
ValueFromRemainingArguments     : False
HelpMessage                     :
HelpMessageBaseName             :
HelpMessageResourceId           :
DontShow                        : False
TypeId                          : System.Management.Automation.ParameterAttribute

AliasNames : {PSPath, LP}
TypeId     : System.Management.Automation.AliasAttribute

PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop> $results = get-process -Name pwsh*
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop> $results | export-clixml

cmdlet Export-Clixml at command pipeline position 1
Supply values for the following parameters:
Path: results.xml
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop>
``` 

### Expected behavior

```console
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop> $results = get-process -Name pwsh*
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop> $results | export-csv

cmdlet Export-Csv at command pipeline position 1
Supply values for the following parameters:
Path: results.csv
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop>
```

### Actual behavior

```console
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop> $results = get-process -Name pwsh*
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop> $results | export-csv
Export-Csv: You must specify either the -Path or -LiteralPath parameters, but not both.
PS C:\Users\xyz\Desktop>
```


title: Release Process for v7.x.x
author: Pegasus691-69
body:
### Checklist

- [ ] Verify that [`PowerShell-Native`](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell-Native) has been updated / released as needed.
- [ ] Check for `PowerShellGet` and `PackageManagement` release plans.
- [ ] Start process to sync Azure DevOps artifacts feed such as modules and NuGet packages.
- [ ] Create a private branch named `release/v6.x.x` in Azure DevOps repository.
  All release related changes should happen in this branch.
- [ ] Prepare packages
    - [ ] Kick off coordinated build.
- [ ] Kick off Release pipeline.
    - *These tasks are orchestrated by the release pipeline, but here as status to the community.*
    - [ ] Prepare packages
        - [ ] Sign the RPM package.
        - [ ] Install and verify the packages.
        - [ ] Trigger the docker staging builds (signing must be done).
    - [ ] Create the release tag and push the tag to `PowerShell/PowerShell` repository.
    - [ ] Run tests on all supported Linux distributions and publish results.
    - [ ] Update documentation, and scripts.
        - [ ] Update [CHANGELOG.md](../../CHANGELOG.md) with the finalized change log draft.
        - [ ] Stage a PR to master to update other documents and
              scripts to use the new package names, links, and `metadata.json`.
    - [ ] For preview releases,
      merge the release branch to GitHub `master` with a merge commit.
    - [ ] For non-preview releases,
      make sure all changes are either already in master or have a PR open.
    - [ ] Delete the release branch.
    - [ ] Trigger the Docker image release.
    - [ ] Retain builds.
    - [ ] Update https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet-docker/tree/master/3.0/sdk with new version and SHA hashes for global tool. NOTE: this link is broken!

title: Visual glitch / text duplication during long input on macOS
author: kda2495
body: ### Prerequisites - [x] Write a descriptive title. - [x] Make sure you are able to repro it on the [latest released version](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases) - [x] Search the existing issues. - [x] Refer to the [FAQ](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/master/docs/FAQ.md). - [x] Refer to [Differences between Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/whats-new/differences-from-windows-powershell). ### Steps to reproduce When using the script on macOS, entering a long sequence of characters (such as multiple IDs) causes a severe visual glitch. Once the input string reaches the right edge of the terminal window, instead of wrapping neatly to the next line, the text starts duplicating and overlapping itself continuously. While the script technically captures the input correctly under the hood, the visual mess makes it impossible for the user to verify what they are typing. Steps to reproduce the behavior: Launch AnyScripy.ps1 on macOS. Select any menu option that prompts for user input (e.g., entering App IDs). Start typing or paste a long string of numbers that exceeds the current width of the terminal window. Observe the terminal failing to wrap the line correctly, causing the text to duplicate multiple times across the screen. ### Expected behavior ```console The terminal should seamlessly wrap the input text to the next line without any visual artifacts or character duplication, just like standard console behavior. ``` ### Actual behavior ```console The console buffer miscalculates the line break, causing the input text to overwrite and duplicate itself repeatedly on the screen, creating visual chaos. ``` ### Error details ```console ``` ### Environment data ```powershell OS: macOS 26.5.2 Terminal: Terminal.app PowerShell version: PowerShell Core (pwsh) 7.6.3 ``` ### Visuals

title: Remove-DnsServerResourceRecord: splatting does not work correctly
author: ahinterl
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] Write a descriptive title.
- [x] Make sure you are able to repro it on the [latest released version](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases)
- [x] Search the existing issues.
- [x] Refer to the [FAQ](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/master/docs/FAQ.md).
- [x] Refer to [Differences between Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/whats-new/differences-from-windows-powershell).

### Steps to reproduce

```
$Arg = @{
    'ComputerName' = 'DC1'
    'ZoneName' = 'mycorp.net'
    'Name' = 'MyComputer'
    'RRType' = 'A'
    'RecordData' = '10.13.19.12'
    'Force' = $true
}

Remove-DnsServerResourceRecord @Arg
```

### Expected behavior

```console
Specified DNS record is deleted, others are left intact.
```

### Actual behavior

```console
All DNS record for the `Name`specified are deleted.
```

### Error details

```console
Workaround: use the non-splatting variant:


Remove-DnsServerResourceRecord -ComputerName 'DC1' -ZoneName 'mycorp.net' -Name $Computername -RRType 'A' -RecordData = $RemoveIPAddress -Force


I get the same incorrect result for:

$Record = Get-DnsServerResourceRecord -ComputerName  'MyComputer' -ZoneName 'mycorp.net' -Name 'MyComputer' | Where-Object { $PSItem.RecordData.IPv4Address.IPAddressToString -eq '10.13.19.12'}

$Arg = @{
    'ComputerName' = 'DC1'
    'ZoneName' = 'mycorp.net'
    'InputObject' = $Record
}

Remove-DnsServerResourceRecord @Arg
```

### Environment data

```powershell
Name                           Value
----                           -----
PSVersion                      7.6.3
PSEdition                      Core
GitCommitId                    7.6.3
OS                             Microsoft Windows 10.0.26200
Platform                       Win32NT
PSCompatibleVersions           {1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0…}
PSRemotingProtocolVersion      2.4
SerializationVersion           1.1.0.1
WSManStackVersion              3.0
```

### Visuals

I execute the PowerShell script in Visual Studio Code 1.27.0.

title: Test-Connection -BufferSize 0 reports buffer size of 32
author: raman118
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Run the `Test-Connection` cmdlet specifying a buffer size of 0, then inspect the `BufferSize` property of the output `PingStatus` object, or check the returned table:

```powershell
$result = Test-Connection -TargetName localhost -Count 1 -BufferSize 0
Write-Host "Result BufferSize property: $($result.BufferSize)"
Write-Host "Actual Reply.Buffer.Length: $($result.Reply.Buffer.Length)"
```

### Expected behavior

The cmdlet output and the `BufferSize` property should correctly reflect the requested buffer size of 0:

```
Result BufferSize property: 0
Actual Reply.Buffer.Length: 0
```

### Actual behavior

The cmdlet output and the `BufferSize` property incorrectly report 32:

```
Result BufferSize property: 32
Actual Reply.Buffer.Length: 0
```

The console output table also reports the `BufferSize (B)` as 32:

```
Ping Source           Address                   Latency BufferSize Status
                                                   (ms)        (B)
---- ------           -------                   ------- ---------- ------
   1 localhost        ::1                             0         32 Success
```

### Error details

No exception is thrown. The ping completes successfully but outputs incorrect metadata.

### Environment data

```
Name                           Value
----                           -----
PSVersion                      7.6.3
PSEdition                      Core
GitCommitId                    7.6.3
OS                             Microsoft Windows 10.0.26200
Platform                       Win32NT
PSCompatibleVersions           {1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0...}
PSRemotingProtocolVersion      2.4
SerializationVersion           1.1.0.1
WSManStackVersion              3.0
```

### Additional Context

This is caused by the following logic in `TestConnectionCommand.cs`:

```csharp
buffer.Length == 0 ? DefaultSendBufferSize : buffer.Length
```

When `-BufferSize 0` is passed, `GetSendBuffer(0)` allocates a 0-length array (`new byte[0]`). Since `buffer.Length` is 0, the expression evaluates to `DefaultSendBufferSize` (32), misreporting the buffer size as 32 bytes even though a 0-byte payload was actually sent.

title: Add the ability to terminate a process tree with `Stop-Process`
author: surfingoldelephant
body:
### Summary of the new feature / enhancement

As a user, I want the ability to terminate a specified process **_and_** any child/descendent processes using the simplest approach in PowerShell (`Stop-Process`). [`taskkill`](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/taskkill) on Windows exposes this type of functionality with a single switch (`/t`). 

`Stop-Process` should have similar ease of use, so that as a user, I'm not required to recursively retrieve and kill processes myself or resort to .NET methods/native commands.

For example, given the following process tree:

```
  Id Name
  -- ----
 220   cmd
6680     conhost
3872     notepad
8356     cmd
8128       notepad
8756       cmd
```

I would like the ability to kill the parent `cmd` process (`220`) and all descendents using a _single_ `Stop-Process` call.

### Proposed technical implementation details (optional)

`Diagnostics.Process` has a [`Kill(Boolean)` overload](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.diagnostics.process.kill#system-diagnostics-process-kill(system-boolean)) (`entireProcessTree`) which supports this functionality.

> Immediately stops the associated process, and optionally its child/descendent processes.

`Stop-Process` already uses `Kill()`:

https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/36cd02b333d7cecb89d5d336c9a798287fe4736b/src/Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.Management/commands/management/Process.cs#L1230-L1233

This could be exposed as a switch on the cmdlet. For example:

- `Stop-Process -Tree`
- `Stop-Process -Recurse`
- `Stop-Process -Children`

title: Add a `SimpleMatch` option to `-replace` to help with literal replacements
author: surfingoldelephant
body:
### Summary of the new feature / enhancement

As a user, I want the ability to opt into simple matching with the `-replace` operator just like I can currently with `-split`.

This would allow me to:

- Use `-replace` without the hassle of having to handle escaping myself, be it manually or by calling `Regex.Escape()` 
  - I.e., when I want to target meta characters for literal replacement.
- Avoid resorting to .NET string methods like `String.Replace()`.
  - These have their own considerations (case-sensitive by default, etc).

`-split` already has a `SimpleMatch` option, which [calls `Regex.Escape()` on the delimiter](https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/blob/eef334de1b0f648512859bd032356f9c8df7cb91/src/System.Management.Automation/engine/lang/parserutils.cs#L731-L753C32) for the user. For consistency, `-replace` should also have this.

### Proposed technical implementation details (optional)

Add a third optional parameter to the RHS of `-replace` (`<options>`). This would accept a value named something such as `SimpleMatch` or `SimpleReplace`.

New [syntax diagram](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_comparison_operators#replacement-operator):
```
<input> -replace <regular-expression>, <substitute>, <options>
```

When the option is specified, `Regex.Escape()` is called on the value of `<regular-expression>` for the user.

```powershell
# This fails due to an invalid regex:
'foo\bar\baz' -replace '\', '/'

# *Currently*, the user must perform escaping.
# Either manually or with Regex.Escape().
'foo\bar\baz' -replace ([regex]::Escape('\')), '/'
# foo/bar/baz

# *After* this request is implemented:
'foo\bar\baz' -replace '\', '/', 'SimpleMatch'
# foo/bar/baz
```

Other considerations:

- `<substitute>` is optional. In order to use `<options>`, the user would be required to at least specify something (`''`/`$null`), just like with other operators.
- Even after escaping, `Regex.Replace()` produces a different result to `String.Replace()` in some cases. It's probably worth documenting that it's still a regex replace performed internally (likewise with `-split`'s `SimpleMatch`).
- Alternatively, a new prefixed operator (e.g., `-lreplace`) could be added for literal replacement instead.

title: Add `EncodingConvertor`
author: iRon7

title: Restore 6 macOS-only test skips from the Pester 5 migration (#27290)
author: nohwnd
body:
The Pester 5 migration (#27290) skips 6 tests on the macOS CI runner only, via `-Skip:$IsMacOS`. They run and pass on Windows and Linux. The differences are in the macOS runner environment / product rendering, not in Pester 5 behavior — the same assertions execute and pass on the other two platforms. Each skip is trivially restorable by removing the `-Skip:$IsMacOS` condition once the underlying issue is fixed.

| Test file | Skipped block | Reason on macOS runner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `engine/Formatting/ErrorView.Tests.ps1` | `ConciseView tests` | ErrorRecord renders as raw `Format-List`, not the Concise view |
| `engine/Formatting/ErrorView.Tests.ps1` | `DetailedView tests` | same formatter-database difference as ConciseView |
| `engine/Formatting/PSStyle.Tests.ps1` | `$PSStyle has correct default for OutputRendering` | non-TTY runner defaults `OutputRendering` to `PlainText` instead of `Host` |
| `engine/Formatting/PSStyle.Tests.ps1` | `$PSStyle.Formatting.CustomTableHeaderLabel is applied to Format-Table` | table-header ANSI sequences not applied |
| `engine/Basic/Encoding.Tests.ps1` | `Using encoding utf7 results in a warning` | warning captured via `3>` renders differently, exact-match fails |
| `engine/Help/HelpSystem.Tests.ps1` | `for module : <name>` | `Get-Help` renders Examples/Syntax/Aliases differently |

To restore: remove `-Skip:$IsMacOS` from each listed block and confirm it passes on the macOS runner.

Follow-up to #27290.

title: Import-Clixml ignores -ErrorAction parameter
author: a-dimas

But it does. Steps to repro: Open a terminal and provide the path to a non-existing file: `Import-Clixml -Path .\there-is-no-spoon -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue` It should produce no output, but you will get the exception ### Expected behavior ```console > Import-Clixml -Path .\there-is-no-spoon -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue PS > ``` ### Actual behavior ```console > Import-Clixml -Path .\there-is-no-spoon -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue PS> Import-Clixml: Could not find file 'C:\Windows\there-is-no-spoon'. ``` ### Error details ```console PS C:\Windows> get-error Exception : Type : System.IO.FileNotFoundException Message : Could not find file 'C:\Windows\there-is-no-spoon'. C:\Users\adima\TMD_Files\Debug> $PSVersionTable Name Value ---- ----- PSVersion 7.6.3 PSEdition Core GitCommitId 7.6.3 OS Microsoft Windows 10.0.26200 Platform Win32NT PSCompatibleVersions {1.0, 2.0, title: Lift the 260 character limit on ntfs drives if using PowerShell on Linux
author: VinVel
body:
### Summary of the new feature / enhancement

When executing `Get-ChildItem` while inside a folder on an ntfs drive, if that folder is very big/has many objects, it errors out with `Get-ChildItem: The path '<path>' is too long, or a component of the specified path is too long.`. 

My understanding is that this is because of a legacy limitation MAX_PATH. However, this legacy limitation does not apply on linux, and ntfs itself (to my knowledge) support more than 260 characters in length.

### Proposed technical implementation details (optional)

_No response_
